goal_summary: Use Kyle's stated morning availability and same-day engagement to secure a specific phone time with Andre for his 08/08/26 wedding.
lead_state_summary: Kyle Melo came in from a Facebook lead form on 05/05 for a wedding on 08/08/26. He replied by SMS at 11:09 saying "Any time in the morning" after being asked the best time for a call, which is the clearest buying signal so far. Since then, there were two outbound call attempts at 13:21, an email at 13:22 with "Comeketo´s Interactive Menu," and a note that live chat started at 10:06 AM. He is already enrolled in active workflows, including "5x5," "✉️ FB Lead Form 1EMAIL," and "01. 📞 DAY 1 CALL," so this plan should avoid duplicating those sequences and focus on converting his stated availability into a booked call.
known_facts: Kyle came in from a Facebook Ads lead form.
An outbound SMS asked for the best time of day to call.
Kyle replied by SMS on 2026-05-05 at 11:09: "Any time in the morning."
Two outbound call attempts were made on 2026-05-05 at 13:21.
An email titled "Comeketo´s Interactive Menu" was sent on 2026-05-05 at 13:22.
A note says live chat started on 2026-05-05 at 10:06 AM.
The lead is already in active workflows and should not be re-enrolled into the same sequence.
unknowns: What specific morning time works best for Kyle for a call with Andre.
Preferred service style for the wedding.
Whether Kyle is the decision-maker or coordinating with a partner/family.
Whether he reviewed the interactive menu email.
best_next_question: Kyle, when you said mornings work, what time tomorrow morning would be easiest for a quick call with Andre so we can understand what you want for 8/8?

days.objective: Convert Kyle's broad morning availability into a specific call time.

required_actions.intent: Ask Kyle to choose a specific morning time for a phone call with Andre
required_actions.draft_seed: Kyle, you mentioned mornings work. What time tomorrow morning is easiest for a quick call with Andre about your 8/8 wedding?
required_actions.notes: Last meaningful inbound was SMS, so lean SMS next. Keep it specific and easy to answer.

days.send_window: SMS between 9:00 AM and 12:00 PM lead-local time

days.objective: If Kyle replies with a time, create a call task; if not, use email to lower friction and offer a simple choice.

required_actions.intent: Call Kyle Melo at the time he selects to confirm needs and move toward a quote
required_actions.draft_seed: If Kyle replies with a morning time, Andre calls then and asks about guest count, venue, and what matters most for the wedding meal.
required_actions.notes: Only execute if Kyle responds with a specific time. No telephony channel allowed, so use task.

required_actions.intent: Offer a simple scheduling choice tied to Kyle's stated morning preference
required_actions.draft_seed:
Subject: Quick call for your wedding

Kyle, you mentioned mornings are best. Would a quick call tomorrow morning or the next morning be easier for you?
required_actions.notes: Use only if no confirmed call time has been set by send time. Distinct intent from day 1 SMS.

days.send_window: Email between 7:30 AM and 10:30 AM lead-local time

days.objective: Clarify one planning detail that naturally leads into a phone conversation.

required_actions.intent: Ask one concrete event-planning question to re-open conversation and support scheduling
required_actions.draft_seed: For your 8/8 wedding, what are you planning around most right now — guest count, menu style, or budget?
required_actions.notes: NEPQ-style question to get a reply without pushing. If he answers, pivot to booking the call.

days.send_window: SMS between 10:00 AM and 1:00 PM lead-local time

days.objective: Have Andre manually review the Box and prepare a tailored call approach based on any new engagement.

required_actions.intent: Review Kyle Melo's live chat, email opens/replies, and SMS history before next outreach
required_actions.draft_seed: Check whether Kyle engaged with the interactive menu or live chat details, then prepare one tailored question for his wedding priorities before the next touch.
required_actions.notes: Keeps the day active without over-messaging and respects frequency caps.

days.send_window: Task for completion between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM lead-local time

days.objective: Re-engage with a low-pressure question that makes the call feel useful, not salesy.

required_actions.intent: Ask a decision-framing question linked to his wedding plans
required_actions.draft_seed:
Subject: For your 8/8 wedding

Kyle, when you picture the food side of the wedding, what do you want to make sure goes right?
required_actions.notes: Short, calm, and designed to invite a real response. If he replies, move to scheduling a call.

days.send_window: Email between 8:00 AM and 11:00 AM lead-local time

days.objective: Make one final SMS attempt this cycle to secure a yes/no on a morning call.

required_actions.intent: Ask for permission to hold a short morning call window with Andre
required_actions.draft_seed: Would it be easier if I had Andre hold a short morning slot for you, or would you rather text a couple details first?
required_actions.notes: Gives Kyle control and two easy paths forward.

days.send_window: SMS between 9:30 AM and 12:00 PM lead-local time

days.objective: Close the cycle with an internal next-step decision based on responsiveness.

required_actions.intent: Assess response pattern and decide whether to continue toward scheduled call or downgrade to longer-term re-engagement
required_actions.draft_seed: If Kyle has replied, schedule/confirm the call with Andre. If silent, note that he stated mornings work, summarize all touches this week, and queue a later re-engagement instead of adding another outbound today.
required_actions.notes: Avoids unnecessary outbound while preserving context for the next cycle.

days.send_window: Task for completion between 10:00 AM and 5:00 PM lead-local time

stop_conditions.trigger: Kyle asks to stop, unsubscribe, or says he is no longer interested
Kyle replies with a specific call time or explicitly agrees to speak with Andre
Kyle asks a direct question about pricing, menu, guest count, or availability
